panels.description:
Load average (avg): System's load average.  

CPU count (avg): System's CPU count available to the process observed.
panels.title: Load average

panels.description:
CPU usage (process) (avg): Process CPU usage.  

CPU usage (system) (avg): CPU Usage of host system.
panels.title: CPU usage (process)

panels.description: Process resident memory size in bytes.
panels.title: Process memory used (rss)

panels.description:
Process files open (avg): Process files opened.  

Process files max (avg): Process files opened limit.
panels.title: Process files open

panels.description:
JVM memory used(heap) (avg): The used space is the amount of memory that is currently occupied by Java objects.  

JVM memory max(heap) (avg): The max heap size is the size specified via the -Xmx flag.
Returns -1 if the maximum memory size is undefined.

This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available for memory management
if it is greater than the amount of committed memory.
The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum size.

See also: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/10/docs/api/java/lang/management/MemoryUsage.html
  

JVM memory committed(heap) (avg): The committed size is the amount of memory guaranteed to be available for use by the Java virtual machine.
panels.title: JVM memory used(heap)

panels.description:
JVM memory used(nonheap) (avg): The amount of memory that is currently in non-heap.  

JVM memory max(nonheap) (avg): Measure of memory max possible (non-heap). Returns -1 if the maximum memory size is undefined.  

JVM memory committed(nonheap) (avg): The committed size is the amount of memory guaranteed to be available for use by the Java virtual machine(non-heap).
panels.title: JVM memory used(nonheap)

panels.description:
Eden Space (used) (avg): Memory used for G1 Eden Space Collection.  

Eden Space (max) (avg): The max heap size is the size specified via the -Xmx flag.
Returns -1 if the maximum memory size is undefined.

This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available for memory management
if it is greater than the amount of committed memory.
The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum size.

See also: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/10/docs/api/java/lang/management/MemoryUsage.html
  

Eden Space (committed) (avg): The committed size is the amount of memory guaranteed to be available for use by the Java virtual machine.
panels.title: Eden Space (used)

panels.description:
Survival space (used) (avg): Memory used for Survival collection.  

Survival space (max) (avg): The max heap size is the size specified via the -Xmx flag.
Returns -1 if the maximum memory size is undefined.

This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available for memory management
if it is greater than the amount of committed memory.
The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum size.

See also: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/10/docs/api/java/lang/management/MemoryUsage.html
  

Survival space (committed) (avg): The committed size is the amount of memory guaranteed to be available for use by the Java virtual machine.
panels.title: Survival space (used)

panels.description:
Tenured space (used) (avg): Memory used for Tenured(Old Gen) collection.  

Tenured space (max) (avg): The max heap size is the size specified via the -Xmx flag.
Returns -1 if the maximum memory size is undefined.

This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available for memory management
if it is greater than the amount of committed memory.
The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum size.

See also: https://docs.oracle.com/javase/10/docs/api/java/lang/management/MemoryUsage.html
  

Tenured space (committed) (avg): The committed size is the amount of memory guaranteed to be available for use by the Java virtual machine.
panels.title: Tenured space (used)

panels.description: Major and minor garbage collections
panels.title: Garbage collections

panels.description:
GC time (avg) (avg): Garbage collection time (max).  

GC time (max) (avg): Garbage collection time (max).

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  avg by (job, action, cause) (
  rate(jvm_gc_pause_sum{job!="",job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])
  /
  rate(jvm_gc_pause_count{job!="",job=~"$job",instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])
)

, "action", "$1", "action", "end of (.+)")

panels.title: GC duration

panels.description:
Allocated (bytes) (avg): Memory allocated to Eden Space.  

Promoted (avg): Memory promoted to Tenured Space.
panels.title: Allocated/promoted

panels.description:
Threads (avg): The current number of live threads including both daemon and non-daemon threads.  

Threads (daemon) (avg): Daemon thread count of a JVM.  

Threads (peak) (avg): Peak thread count of a JVM.  

Threads (deadlocked) (avg): Cycles of JVM-threads that are in deadlock waiting to acquire object monitors or ownable synchronizers.
panels.title: Threads

panels.description: Threads by current state.
panels.title: Threads states

panels.description:
Direct buffer used bytes (avg): Direct buffer is allocated outside the Java heap and represents the OS native memory used by the JVM process. It is generally used for I/O operations. Note that direct buffers aren't freed up by GC.  

Direct buffer capacity (avg): Direct buffer is allocated outside the Java heap and represents the OS native memory used by the JVM process. It is generally used for I/O operations. Note that direct buffers aren't freed up by GC.
panels.title: Direct buffer used bytes

panels.description:
Mapped buffer used (avg): The mapped buffer pool is used for its FileChannel instances.  

Mapped buffer capacity (avg): The mapped buffer pool is used for its FileChannel instances.
panels.title: Mapped buffer used
